message:
feat(abi): port 12 _nss_files_setXX NSS plugin "begin iteration" stubs (bd-ukuxr)

Pair with the endXX stubs from bd-fsknj. All return NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS
(= 1) since we never actually open any backing file. Signatures vary
per family per the NSS files-module convention:

  - No-arg form (nss_files_set_void_stub! macro):
      _nss_files_setaliasent / _nss_files_setetherent
  - `int stayopen` form (nss_files_set_stayopen_stub! macro):
      _nss_files_setgrent / _sethostent / _setnetent / _setprotoent /
      _setpwent / _setrpcent / _setservent / _setsgent / _setspent
  - Special: _nss_files_setnetgrent(*group, *result) — takes the
    netgroup name plus a struct __netgrent result pointer; both
    ignored.

Tests: 3 abi tests covering both no-arg entries, all 9 stayopen
entries (with stayopen=0 and =1), and setnetgrent under both NULL and
arbitrary-arg shapes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-fnm-3","title":"PORT fnmatch.h #3: conformance + GNU extensions parity","description":"Extend conformance_diff_fnmatch_glob.rs with FNM_LEADING_DIR (matches dir prefix), FNM_FILE_NAME (alias for FNM_PATHNAME). Add property-based tests in core (random pattern + text, monotonicity, length bounds). Parent: bd-fnm-epic.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T23:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T00:30:00Z","close_reason":"Extended conformance_diff_fnmatch_glob.rs with 4 new tests: diff_fnmatch_leading_dir (FNM_LEADING_DIR across 7 cases incl 'a/b' vs 'a/b/c/d', confirming pattern matches dir prefixes per glibc), diff_fnmatch_file_name_alias (FNM_FILE_NAME bit alias for FNM_PATHNAME — verifies same behavior across 3 patterns), diff_fnmatch_property_pattern_against_self (literal-escape every metachar in 6 strings, verify pattern matches itself for both impls — property test for escape correctness), diff_fnmatch_star_chain_against_arbitrary_text (7 'star-chain' patterns like '*x*y*z*' against synthetic corpus). All 7 conformance tests pass + 16 core unit tests = 23 tests for fnmatch family. 0 divergences against glibc. Parent: bd-fnm-epic.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fnmatch","porting","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-fnm-epic","title":"PORT: fnmatch.h native Rust impl in frankenlibc-core","description":"fnmatch lives entirely in frankenlibc-abi/src/string_abi.rs (~280 lines: classify_bracket + fnmatch_impl + pub fnmatch wrapper) with all pattern-matching logic inlined alongside the C ABI surface. Pure pattern matching with no syscalls — natural fit for a safe-Rust core port. The port enables independent unit testing of the pattern engine, surfaces the full POSIX surface (literal chars, ?, *, [...], ranges, negation, FNM_PATHNAME, FNM_NOESCAPE, FNM_PERIOD, FNM_CASEFOLD GNU ext), and opens the door for property-based testing. Sub-beads: bd-fnm-1 core engine on byte slices, bd-fnm-2 wire fl::fnmatch to delegate, bd-fnm-3 conformance + GNU FNM_LEADING_DIR / FNM_FILE_NAME parity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-24T23:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T00:30:00Z","close_reason":"Epic complete. All 3 sub-beads closed: bd-fnm-1 (frankenlibc-core/src/string/fnmatch.rs with FnmatchFlags + fnmatch_match() + classify_bracket + fnmatch_inner, 16 unit tests), bd-fnm-2 (replaced ~280 lines of inline fnmatch_impl in fl-abi with 14-line shim delegating to core), bd-fnm-3 (4 conformance regression tests for FNM_LEADING_DIR + FNM_FILE_NAME alias + property tests). Total: 16 core unit tests + 7 conformance tests = 23 tests for the family, 0 divergences against glibc. The fnmatch engine is now native, unit-testable independent of the FFI surface, and uses safe slice-based pure Rust.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","fnmatch","native-rust","porting"]}
 {"id":"bd-frlk1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc fcntl record-locks (F_SETLK/F_GETLK) diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). Tests fcntl with F_SETLK + F_WRLCK (write lock + unlock round-trip), F_RDLCK (read lock), F_GETLK on unlocked region returns F_UNLCK, F_SETLK with NULL flock pointer fails on both. Complements bd-flk1 flock+lockf coverage with byte-range record locks. 5 tests, 0 divergences against glibc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T16:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T16:30:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","fcntl","record-locks"]}
-{"id":"bd-fsknj","title":"PORT: 12 _nss_files_endXX NSS plugin stubs","description":"Add the 12 _nss_files_endXX NSS plugin entries from libc.so.6. These are GLIBC_PRIVATE-versioned NSS plugin "end iteration" callbacks for the nss_files module (the loader that backs /etc/passwd, /etc/group, /etc/hosts, etc. when nsswitch.conf chooses files). Each takes no args and returns enum nss_status. NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS = 1 — "I successfully ended the iteration" — is the safe stub since we never started one. Functions: _nss_files_endaliasent / _endetherent / _endgrent / _endhostent / _endnetent / _endnetgrent / _endprotoent / _endpwent / _endrpcent / _endservent / _endsgent / _endspent. Implementations expose them via a small nss_status_success! macro for compactness.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:13:44.685187614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:13:44.685187614Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

These are GLIBC_PRIVATE-versioned NSS plugin "end iteration" callbacks for the nss_files module (the loader that backs /etc/passwd, /etc/group, /etc/hosts, etc. when nsswitch.conf chooses files). Each takes no args and returns enum nss_status. NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS = 1 — "I successfully ended the iteration" — is the safe stub since we never started one. Functions: _nss_files_endaliasent / _endetherent / _endgrent / _endhostent / _endnetent / _endnetgrent / _endprotoent / _endpwent / _endrpcent / _endservent / _endsgent / _endspent. Implementations expose them via a small nss_status_success! macro for compactness.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:13:44.685187614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:17:46.727875025Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:17:46.725874295Z","close_reason":"shipped: 12 _nss_files_endXX NSS plugin stubs returning NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS via nss_files_end_stub! macro + 1 abi test driving every entry","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ftchp","title":"PORT: unvis (NetBSD vis(3) streaming byte decoder)","description":"NetBSD vis(3) streaming decoder unvis(*cp, c, *astate, flag) — single-byte state-machine inverse of vis(3). Caller feeds bytes one at a time; the function returns UNVIS_VALID (1) when *cp holds a fully decoded byte, UNVIS_VALIDPUSH (2) when *cp is decoded AND the current input byte should be re-fed, UNVIS_NOCHAR (3) for partial sequences, UNVIS_SYNBAD (-1) for malformed input. The opaque *astate carries decoder state across calls. Used by mtree, BSD pkg tools, and anything needing a streaming decoder rather than the all-at-once strunvis. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdio::vis::UnvisDecoder state machine + thin abi shim that maps the i32 state cell to/from the typed enum.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:10:29.760546150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:49:14.754888590Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:49:14.752855201Z","close_reason":"shipped: unvis(3) streaming byte decoder + UnvisDecoder save/restore + 16 abi tests + 3 core tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ftw-1","title":"PORT ftw.h #1: path helpers + WalkType + WalkFlags in frankenlibc-core","description":"Create crates/frankenlibc-core/src/ftw/ module with: WalkType enum (File/Dir/DirNoRead/StatFailed/Symlink/DirPostOrder/DanglingSymlink) bit-compatible with POSIX FTW_F/D/DNR/NS/SL/DP/SLN; WalkFlags struct (physical/depth/mount/chdir) bit-compatible with FTW_PHYS/DEPTH/MOUNT/CHDIR; build_child_path(parent, child_name) -> Vec<u8> helper; base_offset_of(path) -> usize helper for FTW info struct. Pure-safe Rust + comprehensive unit tests. Parent: bd-ftw-epic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T01:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-25T01:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T01:30:00Z","close_reason":"Created crates/frankenlibc-core/src/ftw/path.rs with: WalkType enum (#[repr(i32)] bit-compatible with POSIX FTW_F=0/D=1/DNR=2/NS=3/SL=4/DP=5/SLN=6) + as_c_int(); WalkFlags struct (bit-compat with FTW_PHYS=1/MOUNT=2/CHDIR=4/DEPTH=8) with from_bits/bits/contains + BitOr/BitOrAssign; build_child_path(parent, name) -> Vec<u8> with no-double-slash + empty-parent handling; base_offset_of(path) -> usize for FTW.base. 13 unit tests pass: walktype_numeric_layout_matches_posix, walkflags_bits_match_posix, walkflags_or_combines, walkflags_or_assign, walkflags_from_bits_round_trip, build_child_path_simple/parent_with_trailing_slash/empty_parent/empty_name, base_offset_simple/root/nested/trailing_slash. Also created src/ftw/mod.rs with re-exports. fl-abi rebuilds clean against the new core module. Parent: bd-ftw-epic.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ftw","porting"]}
 {"id":"bd-ftw-2","title":"PORT ftw.h #2: closure-based walker driver in core","description":"Add WalkContext + walk_tree() function operating via closures: lstat/stat/readdir/is_dir/is_symlink/dev_id. Implements pre-order vs post-order (FTW_DEPTH), symlink handling (FTW_PHYS), filesystem boundary check (FTW_MOUNT), and POSIX type-flag dispatch. Pure-safe Rust (no syscalls in core). Unit tests use a mock FsOps implementation. Parent: bd-ftw-epic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T01:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-25T01:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T01:45:00Z","close_reason":"Created crates/frankenlibc-core/src/ftw/walker.rs with: trait StatLike (is_dir/is_symlink/dev_id), trait FsOps (associated Stat: StatLike, stat/lstat/read_dir methods), pub fn walk_tree(root, fs, flags, visit) returning -1 on root-stat fail / first-non-zero visit / 0. Pure-safe Rust. Implements pre-order vs post-order (FTW_DEPTH), symlink classification under FTW_PHYS (Symlink vs DanglingSymlink), filesystem boundary (FTW_MOUNT), DirNoRead on read_dir failure, StatFailed on stat failure (with default-valued stat per POSIX undefined-contents semantics). 7 walker unit tests pass via in-memory MockFs (visits_all_entries, nonexistent_root_returns_minus_one, callback_nonzero_short_circuits, depth_visits_dirs_post_order, mount_skips_other_filesystem, phys_distinguishes_symlinks, unreadable_dir_reports_dirnoread). Total 20 core ftw tests (13 path + 7 walker). fl-abi rebuilds clean. Parent: bd-ftw-epic.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","ftw","porting","walker"]}

 {"id":"bd-ujxkk","title":"PORT: __sigprocmask + __sigwait + __sigwaitinfo + __sigqueue + __pause + __raise + __kill + __killpg + __sigignore + __sighold + __sigrelse (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the signal-management entry points: __sigprocmask, __sigwait, __sigwaitinfo, __sigqueue, __pause, __raise, __kill, __killpg, __sigignore, __sighold, __sigrelse. glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the symbols libstdc++ cancellation cleanup, libpthread internals, and NPTL interruption paths link against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public counterparts in signal_abi (most) and unistd_abi (sigqueue/sigwaitinfo). Tests verify each alias produces the same observable behavior as its public counterpart on a representative input.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:07:34.125693849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:11:53.963332356Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:11:53.961328402Z","close_reason":"shipped: 11 glibc reserved-namespace signal aliases (__sigprocmask/__sigwait/__sigwaitinfo/__sigqueue/__pause/__raise/__kill/__killpg/__sigignore/__sighold/__sigrelse) delegating to existing public-name impls + 5 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Each returns NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS (= 1) since we never actually open any backing file. Signatures vary per family per the NSS files-module convention: setaliasent / setetherent take no args; setgrent / sethostent / setnetent / setprotoent / setpwent / setrpcent / setservent / setsgent / setspent take an int stayopen flag (ignored, retained for ABI compat); setnetgrent takes (const char *netgroup, struct __netgrent *result) and we ignore both. All are exposed via small no-mangle wrappers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:21:50.680454018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:21:50.680454018Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-unbc","title":"syscall: renameat2 invalid flags should override both-null path EFAULT with EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags stay dominant over null-path faults.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:19:53.600277266Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.734480678Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.732334928Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-usntv","title":"PORT: err_set_exit (BSD libutil pre-exit hook)","description":"Port the BSD libutil `err_set_exit(void (*func)(int))` setter that registers a callback invoked by err()/errx()/errc()/verr()/verrx()/verrc() immediately before they call exit(). The hook receives the exit code that err is about to use. Used by daemons/utilities to release locks, flush logs, or close PID files when a fatal error path is taken. Implementation: add a Mutex-protected global cell holding Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(c_int)> in err_abi; expose a setter that swaps and returns the previous value; modify each of the 6 fatal exit sites (err, verr, errx, verrx, errc, verrc) to invoke the hook before sys_exit_group. Tests cover the cell-management contract (set/get/swap), invocation order via a fork-based test that sets a hook writing to a shared pipe before calling errx (parent reads the pipe to confirm hook fired before exit).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:53:33.527097940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:59:06.311110074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:59:06.309078764Z","close_reason":"shipped: err_set_exit pre-exit hook + threading through all 6 fatal exit paths (err/verr/errx/verrx/errc/verrc) + 2 abi tests covering swap-semantics and fork-based hook-fires-before-exit, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
